question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: verifyHashChain()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to accept a forged stream file by supplying a file whose previousHash is empty/null, triggering the SHA_384.isHashEmpty() early-return-true bypass at line 442, so that the hash chain is never validated and incorrect records are silently ingested?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: verifyHashChain()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unconditional true return when expectedPreviousHash is empty (line 443-444) to inject a stream file that breaks the continuity of the hash chain, causing the mirror node to export records that are not contiguous with the canonical ledger state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: verifyHashChain()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence the lastStreamFile AtomicReference (e.g., by causing a restart that clears it) force expectedPreviousHash to be null, bypassing the hash chain check and allowing a gap or substitution of records in the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: verifyHash()] Can an unprivileged external user cause verifyHash() to silently pass by supplying a stream file where both actual and expected hash values are null, since Objects.equals(null, null) returns true at line 432, resulting in a file with no hash integrity check being exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: verifyHash()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a scenario where streamFile.getFileHash() returns null and signature.getFileHashAsHex() also returns null, causing verifyHash() to pass the Objects.equals check and accept a file with no verifiable file hash, leading to incorrect records being exported?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: verifyHash()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the same null-equals-null behavior in the metadata hash check (verifyHash for HASH_TYPE_METADATA) to bypass metadata integrity verification, allowing a file with tampered metadata to be accepted and exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user cause verify() to skip the metadata hash check entirely if streamFile.getMetadataHash() and signature.getMetadataHashAsHex() are both null, since verifyHash() uses Objects.equals and null==null passes, resulting in a file with unverified metadata being exported?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user cause verifySignatures() to return false without throwing an exception (line 382), so that the caller verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles() logs an error but continues processing subsequent files, resulting in a gap in exported records without halting the pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the scenario where all signatures have status other than CONSENSUS_REACHED (line 325), causing verifySignatures() to iterate through all signatures without attempting any download, silently returning false and causing the corresponding stream file to be missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a SignatureVerificationException on every file group except the last one, exploiting the continue-on-exception logic at line 301, so that all intermediate files are skipped and missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a scenario where sigFilesMap contains only one entry that fails signature verification, causing the exception to be re-thrown (line 305) and the entire batch to be abandoned, resulting in missing records in the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that earliestFilename is only set once (line 292-294) and never updated, so that after a batch failure the error message references a stale filename, masking which records are actually missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: downloadNextBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-SignatureVerificationException (e.g., a RuntimeException from a malformed stream file) to be caught by the generic catch at line 167, logged as an error, and silently swallowed, so that the downloader continues from the same position and the affected records are permanently missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: downloadNextBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the DEMO network early-exit path at lines 157-161 by causing sigFilesMap to be empty, which calls downloaderProperties.setEnabled(false) and permanently stops all future downloads, causing all subsequent records to be missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user cause errors on all nodes' signature file downloads, which are silently swallowed by onErrorContinue at line 221, resulting in an empty sigFilesMap and causing the entire batch of records to be missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the onErrorContinue behavior at line 221 to suppress errors from specific nodes, causing those nodes' signatures to be absent from the multimap, potentially preventing consensus from being reached and causing records to be missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the Flux timeout at line 222 to expire before all node signatures are collected, resulting in a partial sigFilesMap where insufficient signatures exist to reach consensus, causing records to be missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: partialCollection()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that partialCollection() uses nodes.getFirst().getTotalStake() (line 507) rather than summing all node stakes, so that if the first node has an incorrect totalStake value, the neededStake calculation is wrong and too few nodes are queried, allowing consensus to be reached with insufficient stake and incorrect records to be exported?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: partialCollection()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Collections.shuffle() randomization at line 505 in partialCollection() to cause non-deterministic node selection, where in some runs a set of colluding nodes is selected that agrees on a forged file hash, causing incorrect records to be exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getStartAfterFilename()] Can an unprivileged external user cause getStartAfterFilename() to return StreamFilename.EPOCH (line 263) by ensuring lastStreamFile is always empty, forcing the downloader to re-download all records from the beginning and potentially overwriting correct records with incorrect ones in the mirror node export?
